extractions.problem: Existing short-read aligners are inefficient or inapplicable for reads >200 bp, and hashing-based tools like BLAT and SSAHA2 are substantially slower in aligned bases per unit time.
extractions.method: BWA-SW algorithm using FM-indices for both reference and query sequences, with dynamic programming between a prefix trie and prefix DAWG, accelerated by heuristic pruning of low-scoring matches and seed containment.
extractions.finding: BWA-SW is as accurate as SSAHA2, more accurate than BLAT, and several to tens of times faster than both, with dramatically lower alignment error rates especially for longer reads.
extractions.dataset: Simulated reads from human genome (100 bp–10,000 bp at 2–10% error rates, ~10 Mbp total per condition)
extractions.metric: CPU time (seconds), Q20% (fraction confidently aligned), errAln% (alignment error rate)
extractions.result: BWA-SW errAln 0.00–0.17% vs BLAT 0.00–5.47%; 3–40x faster than BLAT, 10–80x faster than SSAHA2

extractions.problem: Understanding the biological processes generating somatic mutations in cancer is limited despite all cancers being caused by somatic mutations.
extractions.method: Extracted mutational signatures from a 96-class substitution classification (6 base substitution types × 16 trinucleotide contexts) across 7,042 cancers using computational decomposition, validated by orthogonal sequencing and visual curation.
extractions.finding: Identified 21 validated distinct mutational signatures; some are ubiquitous (Signatures 1A/1B in 25/30 cancer types), others are cancer-type specific; discovered localized hypermutation (kataegis) in many cancer types.
extractions.dataset: 4,938,362 mutations from 7,042 cancers of 30 classes (507 whole-genome, 6,535 exome sequences)
extractions.metric: Signature validation rate, mutation prevalence per Mb
extractions.result: 21 of 27 signatures validated; mutation prevalence ranges from ~0.001/Mb to >400/Mb across cancer types

extractions.problem: Accurate prediction of MHC class I-peptide binding affinity across multiple alleles and peptide lengths (8–11 residues) for immunological applications.
extractions.method: Artificial neural networks trained on quantitative affinity data from IEDB and eluted peptide data from SYFPEITHI for 55 MHC alleles, supplemented by position-specific scoring matrices for 67 additional HLA alleles, with a length approximation algorithm.
extractions.finding: NetMHC-3.0 achieves high prediction accuracy validated on a large independent dataset, with 75–80% confirmed MHC binders in pathogen proteome predictions.
extractions.dataset: IEDB + SYFPEITHI training data; 6,452 non-redundant 9-mer affinity measurements covering 32 HLA alleles for evaluation
extractions.metric: AUC, Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC), sensitivity, positive predictive value (PPV)
extractions.baseline: Previous NetMHC versions, directly trained 10-mer ANNs
extractions.result: AUC=0.86, PCC=0.71 for 9-mers; 76% sensitivity, 78% PPV at IC50<500 nM threshold

extractions.problem: Early prediction of drug-induced pro-arrhythmic cardiotoxicity is critical, but current animal models have limited accuracy and raise ethical and translational concerns.
extractions.method: In silico drug trials in a population of 1,213 human ventricular action potential models (ORd-based) for 62 reference compounds using pore-block IC50/Hill coefficient drug models, with comparison against rabbit wedge ECGs and hiPS-CM recordings.
extractions.finding: Repolarization abnormalities in the population of models predict clinical TdP risk with higher accuracy than single-model biomarkers or animal models; drug-induced changes are consistent across in silico, ex vivo, and in vitro assays.
extractions.dataset: 62 reference compounds, 1,213 human ventricular AP models, rabbit wedge ECGs, hiPS-CM Ca2+-transient recordings
extractions.metric: Accuracy, sensitivity, specificity for clinical TdP risk classification
extractions.baseline: Single ORd model (17% sensitivity), APD prolongation-based classification (80% accuracy, 64% specificity)
extractions.result: 89% accuracy overall; 96% accuracy, 100% sensitivity, 92% specificity for high-risk vs safe drug classification

extractions.problem: Genome-scale kinetic models of metabolism that faithfully recapitulate effects of multiple genetic interventions are needed for reliable design of overproducing microbial strains.
extractions.method: Ensemble modeling approach with elementary kinetic representations, parameterized by a genetic algorithm simultaneously fitting fluxomic data from wild-type and 25 mutant strains across multiple growth conditions; 457 reactions, 337 metabolites, 295 regulatory interactions.
extractions.finding: k-ecoli457 achieves substantially higher correlation with experimental product yields than FBA, MOMA, or maximum yield approaches, with 61% of predicted fluxes within experimental error ranges.
extractions.dataset: E. coli wild-type and 25 mutant strains (4 growth conditions), 320 engineered strains spanning 24 product metabolites
extractions.metric: Pearson correlation coefficient, fraction of predicted fluxes within experimental ranges
extractions.baseline: FBA (r=0.18), MOMA (r=0.37), maximization of product yield (r=0.47)
extractions.result: r=0.84 for product yields across 320 strains; 61% of fluxes within 1 SD of experimental data

extractions.problem: Quantifying how metabolic constraints and transcriptional regulation each contribute to determining metabolic gene activity in an integrated genome-scale model.
extractions.method: Steady-state regulatory flux balance analysis (SR-FBA) using mixed integer linear programming to identify metabolic-regulatory steady states, with flux variability analysis to explore alternative solutions.
extractions.finding: Metabolic constraints determine 45–51% of gene flux activity states while transcriptional regulation determines 13–20%; 36 genes are redundantly expressed; ~30% remain undetermined, suggesting metabolic variability.
extractions.dataset: E. coli integrated metabolic-regulatory model under 110 minimal growth media (66 aerobic, 44 anaerobic)
extractions.metric: Fraction of genes with determined expression/activity states, gene expression prediction accuracy
extractions.baseline: Standard FBA (metabolic constraints only), Boolean regulatory model alone
extractions.result: 45–51% determined by metabolism, 13–20% by regulation, 36 redundantly expressed genes, ~30% undetermined

extractions.problem: Testing whether contractile myocytes sense their mechanical microenvironment and alter differentiation, organization, and morphology accordingly.
extractions.method: C2C12 myoblasts cultured on collagen-coated polyacrylamide gels of varied elasticity (1–100+ kPa); AFM indentation of excised normal (C57) and dystrophic (mdx) mouse EDL muscle; immunofluorescence for myosin/actin striations.
extractions.finding: Myosin/actin striations emerge only on substrates matching normal muscle stiffness (~12 kPa); not on glass, much softer, or stiffer gels including dystrophic-stiffness gels; adhesion strength increases monotonically with substrate stiffness.
extractions.dataset: C2C12 myoblasts, C57 normal and mdx dystrophic mouse EDL muscle (n=387 AFM indentations)
extractions.metric: Young's modulus (E, kPa), striation presence/absence, cell area, adhesion strength
extractions.result: E_normal=12±4 kPa, E_mdx=18±6 kPa; striations only at ~12 kPa matching tissue stiffness

extractions.problem: Predicting B-cell epitopes from antigen sequences alone has seen limited accuracy improvement over the past decade, especially for sequence-only methods.
extractions.method: Random forest regression trained on epitopes annotated from 160 non-redundant antibody-antigen crystal structures, using residue physicochemical properties and NetSurfP-predicted structural features in a sliding window.
extractions.finding: BepiPred-2.0 significantly outperforms BepiPred-1.0 and LBtope on both structural epitope benchmarks and IEDB linear epitope data; training on structurally defined epitopes improves performance even for linear epitope prediction.
extractions.dataset: 160 non-redundant antigen-antibody crystal structures (PDB), IEDB linear epitope collection
extractions.metric: AUC, AUC10%, positive predictive value (PPV), true positive rate (TPR)
extractions.result: AUC=0.62, AUC10%=0.121 on structural epitopes (p<1e-6 vs all baselines)

extractions.problem: Invasive tumor dissemination is only incompletely attenuated by protease inhibitors, suggesting unknown compensatory migration mechanisms.
extractions.method: 3D collagen matrix migration assays with broad-spectrum protease inhibitor cocktail (MMP, serine, cathepsin inhibitors); time-lapse video and confocal microscopy in vitro; intravital multiphoton microscopy in mouse dermis in vivo.
extractions.finding: Near-total protease inhibition induces a mesenchymal-to-amoeboid transition (MAT) with near-undiminished migration rates; cells switch from path-generating proteolytic to path-finding amoeboid squeezing through matrix gaps.
extractions.dataset: HT-1080/MT1 fibrosarcoma cells, MDA-MB-231 breast carcinoma cells, mouse dermis (in vivo)
extractions.metric: Migration speed, morphology transition percentage (mesenchymal vs amoeboid), collagen degradation
extractions.baseline: Untreated proteolytic mesenchymal migration (no protease inhibitors)
extractions.result: Near-undiminished migration rates under protease inhibition; conversion from spindle to amoeboid morphology

extractions.problem: Providing a comprehensive, publicly accessible catalog of experimentally characterized immune epitopes to support immunology research and vaccine development.
extractions.method: Manual curation by PhD-level curators from published literature following standardized guidelines, combined with direct investigator data submissions; complete redesign of database structure and querying interface for IEDB 2.0.
extractions.finding: IEDB 2.0 covers ~99% of publicly available peptide epitope data for infectious agents (excluding HIV) and 93% for allergens, with both positive and negative experimental results captured.
extractions.dataset: 180,978 literature-curated experiments + 129,186 directly submitted experiments across all tested species
extractions.metric: Coverage percentage of publicly available epitope data
extractions.result: ~99% coverage for infectious agent epitopes, 93% for allergen epitopes
